title: A side-by-side comparison shows stark differences between Meta and Anthropic's AI ad campaigns
excerpt: Anthropic and Meta premiered rival ads, both of which reference AI worries and offer hope — but with key differences in their visuals and messaging.
content_html:
By Henry Chandonnet

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

There's another AI advertising skirmish brewing.

Meta debuted a new ad campaign on Thursday with a spot promising that the "future is for everyone," complete with fairies and butterflies. "Meta has always believed in giving people the power to share, connect, and shape your world in the ways you want," CEO Mark Zuckerberg wrote.

It's hot on the heels of Anthropic's "hard questions" ad two weeks ago that raised eye-brows with its bleak imagery. Some AI fans thought that it was overly pessimistic or "doomer," opening with a house burning to the ground. Sam Altman wrote on X that he initially thought the ad was "satire" from a fake account.

There's no direct call-out of Anthropic in the Meta ad, and Meta began working on its campaign long before Anthropic's ad debuted, a person familiar with the matter said. Still, its structure is strikingly similar, even if the imagery stands in stark contrast. AI companies have brawled in ads before, like the Super Bowl battle between Anthropic and OpenAI.

Here a side-by-side comparison (Anthropic's ad on the left, Meta's on the right) highlighting the similarities — and key differences.

Both ads address AI anxiety.

It's no secret: AI often elicits fear, anxiety, and boos.

The first half of Anthropic's ad features questions about these worries. "Can AI be trusted?" one voice asked. "Who's gonna hit the brakes if we need to?" another asked. (Anthropic also clarified that all of these voices were human, not AI.)

The Meta ad also references these concerns, but only for a few seconds. There are flashes of headlines, warning about AI job losses. The tone also sounds a bit accusatory: "Some people will have you believe AI will make us less connected."

Anthropic stays on sour images, while Meta brightens up.

Shortly after referencing the negative headlines, the Meta ad changes to a colorful beam of positivity. There are rainbows! Hugs! Children with butterflies!

Meanwhile, the Anthropic ad lingers on those worries. Over a stilted, staccato piano soundtrack, the ad flashes to photos of homelessness and alludes to death with an imagery of a cemetery.

You can see the tonal difference in the (hidden) faces.

One clear difference between Anthropic and Meta's ads: the faces shown.

The Anthropic ad flashes a man with his hands on his head, possibly in pain or stress — or at least deep in thought. Meanwhile, the Meta ad flashes to arched eyebrows and wide eyes. One can imagine that, if the camera panned down, they'd be smiling.

Meta is clearer about its product placement than Anthropic.

You could watch the Anthropic ad and not realize what company it was for, until the Claude logo flashed at the end.

Anthropic's product placement is relatively subdued. There are a few shots of people in front of computers, where Claude is visible but blurry. The clearest reference is the Claude stickers on a laptop, but those are partially cut out of the shot.

Meta's logos are all over its ad. There are references to Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp. At one point, the ad shows Meta's new AI model name in full: Muse Spark 1.1.

Both ads come around to celebration.

While it takes a bit longer, the Anthropic ad eventually strikes a hopeful tone. The voices ask about becoming better teachers and parents, thanks to AI. There are delightful shots of people spraying water into the street.

Meanwhile, Meta's joy intensifies. There are celebrities, like Jalen Brunson and Kylie Jenner (Meta partnered with Jenner for its latest AI glasses). There are more shots of children.

People might find Anthropic's message muddled.

The final line of Anthropic's ad strikes both tones.

The first two words are positive, about "hope." It brings back the joy we saw, the whale flying through the sky. The close is "hard questions," a reminder of the ad's opening. Those worries — the job losses, the burning houses and cemeteries — won't go away.

It's a tricky balance to try to strike in an ad, and it makes sense that it left some people saying they found it muddled or confusing.

Meta's message is (not shockingly) positive.

Meta's ad ends on a high note.

"The future is for everyone," it commands. It promises us equal access and usefulness. But there's no reference to those worrying headlines earlier in the ad. They seem to have washed away, replaced by one big smile.

Read next

Henry Chandonnet

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

  • Anthropic
  • Meta
  • Mark Zuckerberg
  • More

blocks.html_en: Meta's message is (not shockingly) positive.

sentences.en: Meta's message is (not shockingly) positive.
sentences.zh: 梅塔传达的信息是积极的（并不令人震惊）。

sentences.en: Meta's final line says that the "future is for everyone."
sentences.zh: Meta 的最后一句话是“未来属于每个人”。

sentences.en: Screenshot via Meta
sentences.zh: 截图来自元

blocks.html_en: Meta's ad ends on a high note.

sentences.en: Meta's ad ends on a high note.
sentences.zh: Meta 的广告以高调结束。

blocks.html_en: "The future is for everyone," it commands. It promises us equal access and usefulness. But there's no reference to those worrying headlines earlier in the ad. They seem to have washed away, replaced by one big smile.

sentences.en: "The future is for everyone," it commands.
sentences.zh: 它命令道：“未来属于每个人。”

sentences.en: It promises us equal access and usefulness.
sentences.zh: 它向我们承诺平等的机会和有用性。

sentences.en: But there's no reference to those worrying headlines earlier in the ad.
sentences.zh: 但广告中没有提到前面那些令人担忧的头条新闻。

sentences.en: They seem to have washed away, replaced by one big smile.
sentences.zh: 它们似乎已经被冲走，取而代之的是灿烂的笑容。

content_html_zh:
作者：Henry Chandonnet 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

另一场人工智能广告冲突正在酝酿之中。

Meta 于周四推出了新的广告活动，其中一个广告承诺“未来属于每个人”，其中有仙女和蝴蝶。 首席执行官马克·扎克伯格写道：“Meta 始终相信赋予人们以自己想要的方式分享、联系和塑造世界的能力。”

两周前，Anthropic 发布了“难题”广告，广告中惨淡的画面令人大吃一惊，随后该广告就引起了热议。 一些人工智能粉丝认为这过于悲观或“厄运”，以房屋被烧毁为开场。 萨姆·奥尔特曼 (Sam Altman) 在 X 上写道，他最初认为该广告是来自虚假帐户的“讽刺”。

一位知情人士称，Meta 广告中没有直接提及 Anthropic，而且早在 Anthropic 广告首次亮相之前，Meta 就开始开展宣传活动。 尽管如此，尽管图像形成鲜明对比，但其结构却惊人相似。 人工智能公司之前也曾在广告中发生过争执，比如 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 之间的超级碗大战。

这是一个并排比较（Anthropic 的广告在左边，Meta 的广告在右边）突出了相似之处和关键差异。

这两则广告都解决了人工智能的焦虑问题。

这不是什么秘密：人工智能经常引起恐惧、焦虑和嘘声。

Anthropic 广告的前半部分提出了有关这些担忧的问题。 “人工智能可以信任吗？” 一个声音问道。 “如果需要的话，谁来踩刹车？” 另一个问道。 （Anthropic 还澄清，所有这些声音都是人类的，而不是人工智能。）

元广告也提到了这些问题，但只持续了几秒钟。 不断出现的头条新闻警告人工智能将导致失业。 语气听起来也有点指责：“有些人会让你相信人工智能会让我们的联系减少。”

Anthropic 停留在酸涩的图像上，而 Meta 则变得明亮起来。

在引用负面头条新闻后不久，元广告就变成了彩色的积极信息。 有彩虹！ 拥抱！ 孩子们与蝴蝶！

与此同时，人性化的广告却挥之不去，挥之不去。 广告在生硬、断断续续的钢琴配乐中，闪现出无家可归的照片，并用墓地的意象暗示死亡。

您可以看到（隐藏的）面孔中的色调差异。

Anthropic 和 Meta 广告之间的一个明显区别是：所显示的面孔。

人性化广告中出现了一个男人双手放在头上的画面，他可能正处于痛苦或压力之中，或者至少正在陷入沉思。 与此同时，元广告闪烁着拱起的眉毛和睁大的眼睛。 人们可以想象，如果镜头平移，他们会微笑。

Meta 比 Anthropic 更清楚其植入式广告。

你可能在观看 Anthropic 广告时并不知道它是哪家公司的，直到克劳德标志在最后闪现。

Anthropic 的植入式广告相对较少。 有几张人们在电脑前的照片，克劳德清晰可见，但很模糊。 最清晰的参考是笔记本电脑上的克劳德贴纸，但这些贴纸被部分地从镜头中剪掉了。

Meta 的标志遍布其广告中。 其中提到了 Facebook、Instagram 和 WhatsApp。 广告中有一次展示了 Meta 的新 AI 模型的完整名称：Muse Spark 1.1。

这两个广告都是为了庆祝。

虽然需要更长的时间，但 Anthropic 广告最终呈现出充满希望的基调。 这些声音要求借助人工智能，成为更好的老师和家长。 有一些令人愉快的镜头，人们向街上喷水。

与此同时，梅塔的喜悦更加强烈。 其中包括杰伦·布伦森 (Jalen Brunson) 和凯莉·詹纳 (Kylie Jenner) 等名人（Meta 与詹纳合作推出了最新的人工智能眼镜）。 还有更多孩子们的镜头。

人们可能会觉得 Anthropic 的信息很混乱。

Anthropic 广告的最后一句话同时采用了两种语气。

前两个词是积极的，关于“希望”。 它带回了我们看到的欢乐，鲸鱼在天空中飞翔。 结尾是“难题”，提醒人们注意广告的开头。 这些担忧——失业、房屋和墓地被烧毁——不会消失。

在广告中试图达到一个微妙的平衡是有道理的，它让一些人说他们发现它混乱或令人困惑，这是有道理的。

梅塔传达的信息是积极的（并不令人震惊）。

Meta 的广告以高调结束。

它命令道：“未来属于每个人。” 它向我们承诺平等的机会和有用性。 但广告中没有提到前面那些令人担忧的头条新闻。 它们似乎已经被冲走，取而代之的是灿烂的笑容。
